message:
test: add comprehensive test suites for glamour, glow, harmonica, lipgloss, and wish crates

New test files expanding coverage across multiple charmed_rust crates:

- glamour: markdown_edge_cases.rs — edge case tests for markdown rendering
- glow: browser_tests.rs — browser interaction and rendering tests
- harmonica: physics_edge_cases.rs — spring/easing physics boundary tests
- lipgloss: layout_and_ranges_tests.rs — layout composition and range tests
- lipgloss: layout_property_tests.rs — property-based layout verification
- wish: types_and_session_tests.rs — SSH type definitions and session tests

Also syncs .beads/issues.jsonl tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ywx","title":"glamour: Validate language aliases on registration","description":"# Task: Validate Language Aliases on Registration

## Problem Statement
The language_alias() builder method accepts any strings without validation:

```rust
// Lines 623-630
pub fn language_alias(mut self, alias: impl Into<String>, language: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    self.syntax_config
        .language_aliases
        .insert(alias.into(), language.into());
    self
}
```

No checks for:
1. Whether target language is actually supported
2. Whether alias forms a cycle (A -> B -> A)
3. Whether alias shadows a built-in language name

## Impact Analysis
- **Silent failures**: Alias to non-existent language just doesn't highlight
- **Confusing behavior**: Cycles could cause infinite loops or unexpected results
- **Debugging difficulty**: No error message explains why highlighting isn't working

## Solution Design

### Validation on Registration
```rust
pub fn language_alias(
    mut self,
    alias: impl Into<String>,
    language: impl Into<String>,
) -> Result<Self, GlamourError> {
    let alias = alias.into();
    let language = language.into();
    
    // Check target language exists
    if !self.syntax_set_contains(&language) {
        return Err(GlamourError::InvalidLanguage(language));
    }
    
    // Check for cycles
    if self.would_create_cycle(&alias, &language) {
        return Err(GlamourError::AliasCycle { alias, language });
    }
    
    self.syntax_config.language_aliases.insert(alias, language);
    Ok(self)
}

fn would_create_cycle(&self, alias: &str, target: &str) -> bool {
    let mut current = target;
    let mut visited = HashSet::new();
    visited.insert(alias);
    
    while let Some(next) = self.syntax_config.language_aliases.get(current) {
        if !visited.insert(next.as_str()) {
            return true;  // Cycle detected
        }
        current = next;
    }
    false
}
```

### Backward Compatibility Option
Keep existing method but add validated version:
```rust
// Existing (for compatibility)
pub fn language_alias(self, alias: impl Into<String>, language: impl Into<String>) -> Self

// New validated version
pub fn try_language_alias(
    self,
    alias: impl Into<String>,
    language: impl Into<String>,
) -> Result<Self, GlamourError>
```

## Implementation Steps
1. Add validation logic to language_alias()
2. Add GlamourError variants for invalid language and cycles
3. Consider deprecating unchecked version
4. Update documentation with valid language list
5. Add tests for validation

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn alias_to_invalid_language_fails() {
    let result = GlamourRenderer::new()
        .try_language_alias("rs", "nonexistent");
    assert!(matches!(result, Err(GlamourError::InvalidLanguage(_))));
}

#[test]
fn alias_cycle_detected() {
    let result = GlamourRenderer::new()
        .try_language_alias("a", "b")?
        .try_language_alias("b", "c")?
        .try_language_alias("c", "a");  // Creates cycle
    assert!(matches!(result, Err(GlamourError::AliasCycle { .. })));
}

#[test]
fn valid_alias_succeeds() {
    let result = GlamourRenderer::new()
        .try_language_alias("rs", "rust");
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Invalid target language detected with clear error
- [ ] Alias cycles detected and rejected
- [ ] Backward-compatible API (old method still works)
- [ ] Documentation lists valid language names
- [ ] Tests cover validation scenarios","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:13.883578694Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:05:45.211031393Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:05:45.210961983Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["configuration","glamour","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ywx","depends_on_id":"bd-8wlh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:13.902020466Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-244a","title":"glamour: Add LRU cache limit to StyleCache","description":"# Task: Add Size Limit to StyleCache

## Problem Statement
The StyleCache in syntax.rs uses unbounded Vec storage:

\`\`\`rust
pub struct StyleCache {
    cache: Vec<(SynStyle, LipglossStyle)>,  // No size limit!
}

impl StyleCache {
    pub fn get_or_convert(&mut self, syn_style: &SynStyle) -> &LipglossStyle {
        // Linear search O(n)
        if let Some(idx) = self.cache.iter().position(|(s, _)| s == syn_style) {
            return &self.cache[idx].1;
        }
        // Add new entry - unbounded growth
        self.cache.push((syn_style.clone(), lip_style));
        &self.cache.last().unwrap().1
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Impact Analysis
- **Memory**: Large code files with many unique styles accumulate entries
- **Performance**: Linear search degrades as cache grows
- **No cleanup**: Entries never removed, even for completed documents

## Solution Design

### Use LRU Cache with Fixed Size
\`\`\`rust
use lru::LruCache;
use std::num::NonZeroUsize;

pub struct StyleCache {
    cache: LruCache<SynStyle, LipglossStyle>,
}

impl StyleCache {
    const DEFAULT_CAPACITY: usize = 256;
    
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            cache: LruCache::new(NonZeroUsize::new(Self::DEFAULT_CAPACITY).unwrap()),
        }
    }
    
    pub fn with_capacity(capacity: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            cache: LruCache::new(NonZeroUsize::new(capacity.max(1)).unwrap()),
        }
    }
    
    pub fn get_or_convert(&mut self, syn_style: &SynStyle) -> LipglossStyle {
        if let Some(style) = self.cache.get(syn_style) {
            return style.clone();
        }
        let lip_style = convert_style(syn_style);
        self.cache.put(syn_style.clone(), lip_style.clone());
        lip_style
    }
    
    /// Returns the current number of cached entries.
    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
        self.cache.len()
    }
    
    /// Returns true if the cache is empty.
    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
        self.cache.is_empty()
    }
    
    /// Clears all cached entries.
    pub fn clear(&mut self) {
        self.cache.clear();
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Why LRU?
- Simple API, well-tested crate
- O(1) get and put operations
- Automatic eviction of least-recently-used entries
- Common styles (comments, keywords) stay cached

## Implementation Steps
1. Add \`lru\` crate as dependency
2. Replace Vec with LruCache
3. Update get_or_convert to use LRU API (returns clone, not reference)
4. Add configurable capacity
5. Add len(), is_empty(), clear() methods
6. Add benchmarks comparing old vs new
7. Test cache eviction behavior

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod style_cache_tests {
    use super::*;

    fn make_style(id: u8) -> SynStyle {
        SynStyle {
            foreground: syntect::highlighting::Color { r: id, g: 0, b: 0, a: 255 },
            background: syntect::highlighting::Color::WHITE,
            font_style: syntect::highlighting::FontStyle::empty(),
        }
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_default_capacity() {
        let cache = StyleCache::new();
        assert_eq!(cache.capacity(), 256);
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_custom_capacity() {
        let cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(100);
        // Note: actual capacity might be slightly different due to LRU internals
        assert!(cache.capacity() >= 100);
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_respects_limit() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(3);
        
        // Add 4 different styles
        for i in 0..4 {
            let style = make_style(i);
            cache.get_or_convert(&style);
        }
        
        // Cache should have exactly 3 entries (oldest evicted)
        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 3);
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_hit_returns_same_style() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(10);
        let style = make_style(42);
        
        let first = cache.get_or_convert(&style);
        let second = cache.get_or_convert(&style);
        
        // Should return identical styles
        assert_eq!(first, second);
    }

    #[test]
    fn lru_eviction_behavior() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(2);
        
        let s1 = make_style(1);
        let s2 = make_style(2);
        let s3 = make_style(3);
        
        cache.get_or_convert(&s1);  // [s1]
        cache.get_or_convert(&s2);  // [s1, s2]
        cache.get_or_convert(&s1);  // [s2, s1] - s1 moved to back (recently used)
        cache.get_or_convert(&s3);  // [s1, s3] - s2 evicted (LRU)
        
        // s1 should still be cached (recently used)
        // s2 should be evicted (least recently used)
        // s3 should be cached (just added)
        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 2);
        
        // Verify by checking if lookup requires conversion
        // (This is an indirect test - ideally track cache misses)
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_clear() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(10);
        cache.get_or_convert(&make_style(1));
        cache.get_or_convert(&make_style(2));
        
        assert!(!cache.is_empty());
        
        cache.clear();
        
        assert!(cache.is_empty());
        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 0);
    }

    #[test]
    fn many_unique_styles_bounded() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(100);
        
        // Add 1000 unique styles
        for i in 0u8..=255 {
            for j in 0u8..4 {
                let style = SynStyle {
                    foreground: syntect::highlighting::Color { r: i, g: j, b: 0, a: 255 },
                    background: syntect::highlighting::Color::WHITE,
                    font_style: syntect::highlighting::FontStyle::empty(),
                };
                cache.get_or_convert(&style);
            }
        }
        
        // Cache should be bounded at capacity
        assert!(cache.len() <= 100);
    }
}

\`\`\`rust
use criterion::{criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion, BenchmarkId};

fn bench_cache_hits(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(256);
    let style = make_style(42);
    cache.get_or_convert(&style);  // Prime cache
    
    c.bench_function("cache_hit", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            cache.get_or_convert(&style)
        });
    });
}

fn bench_cache_misses(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(256);
    let mut counter = 0u8;
    
    c.bench_function("cache_miss", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            counter = counter.wrapping_add(1);
            cache.get_or_convert(&make_style(counter))
        });
    });
}

fn bench_cache_under_pressure(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let mut group = c.benchmark_group("cache_pressure");
    
    for capacity in [16, 64, 256, 1024] {
        group.bench_with_input(
            BenchmarkId::new("lookup", capacity),
            &capacity,
            |b, &cap| {
                let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(cap);
                let mut counter = 0u8;
                
                b.iter(|| {
                    counter = counter.wrapping_add(1);
                    cache.get_or_convert(&make_style(counter))
                });
            },
        );
    }
    group.finish();
}

criterion_group!(benches, bench_cache_hits, bench_cache_misses, bench_cache_under_pressure);
criterion_main!(benches);

    fi

echo ""

if grep -q "FAILED" /tmp/style_cache.log; then

    exit 1
else
    echo "[PASS] All StyleCache tests passed"
fi

 {"id":"bd-ygt5","title":"Property Tests: bubbles component state machines","description":"Property-based tests for bubbles component state invariants.

## Purpose
Use proptest to verify state machine invariants hold
for all possible input sequences.

## Properties to Test

### Viewport
- [ ] scroll_position always in [0, max_scroll]
- [ ] visible_lines <= content_lines
- [ ] viewport_top + visible_lines <= content_lines

### Progress
- [ ] percentage always in [0.0, 1.0]
- [ ] set_percent clamps out-of-range values
- [ ] width never negative

### TextInput
- [ ] cursor_position in [0, content_length]
- [ ] cursor never beyond content
- [ ] delete at position 0 is no-op
- [ ] insert increases length by 1

### Spinner
- [ ] frame_index always in [0, frame_count)
- [ ] tick advances by 1 (mod frame_count)
- [ ] custom frames work same as built-in

### List
- [ ] selected_index in [0, items.len()) or None
- [ ] visible_items <= items.len()
- [ ] filter matches subset of items

## Test Strategy

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn viewport_scroll_stays_bounded(
        content_lines in 1usize..1000,
        viewport_height in 1usize..100,
        scroll_ops in prop::collection::vec(0i32..10, 0..50),
    ) {
        let mut vp = Viewport::new(content_lines, viewport_height);
        for op in scroll_ops {
            vp.scroll(op);
            assert!(vp.scroll_position() <= vp.max_scroll());
        }
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- State invariants always hold
- No panic for any input sequence
- Edge cases (empty, max) handled","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:21.619524500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:59.683687463Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","property","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ygt5","depends_on_id":"bd-9sjn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:59.683652437Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ygt5","depends_on_id":"bd-ndn1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:21.653068222Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-yqh4","title":"demo_showcase: flagship 'turned up to 11' showcase binary","description":"Create a new standalone Rust binary crate (`crates/demo_showcase`) that acts as the *flagship* demo app for the whole charmed_rust ecosystem.

Why:
- Prove end-to-end functionality across crates in one realistic, cohesive application.
- Provide a visually stunning, highly polished reference implementation ("how to build real apps with this stack").
- Serve as a regression surface: if the demo stays amazing, the library is healthy.

What this demo must showcase (integrated, not toy snippets):
- bubbletea: model/update/view architecture, commands, timers, resize handling, mouse events, focus, paste, async mode (feature-gated), graceful exit/terminal restore.
- lipgloss: layout (join/place), borders, width/height, alignment, wrapping, truncation, theming + live theme switching.
- bubbles: multiple components working together (viewport, list, table, progress, spinner, textinput/textarea, filepicker, paginator, timer/stopwatch, help).
- glamour: markdown rendering with themes + syntax highlighting (when enabled), width-aware wrapping, link formatting.
- huh: real form flows (validation, multi-step, confirm/select) embedded as an in-app workflow.
- wish (optional runtime mode): run the same app over SSH (or a subset) to prove SSH viability.
- charmed_log: styled logging output and/or an in-app log viewer.
- harmonica: animations/transitions (numbers, progress easing, panel transitions).

## Parent Epic
charmed_rust-idi: Add Syntax Highlighting to Glamour

## Objective
Extend glamour's StyleConfig to allow users to select syntax highlighting themes and customize highlighting behavior through the existing configuration API.

## Detailed Requirements

### 1. Extend StyleConfig
```rust
/// Configuration for glamour rendering
pub struct StyleConfig {
    // ... existing fields
    
    /// Syntax highlighting theme (when feature enabled)
    #[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
    pub syntax_theme: SyntaxThemeConfig,
}

#[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
pub struct SyntaxThemeConfig {
    /// Theme name (e.g., "base16-ocean.dark")
    pub theme: String,
    /// Whether to show line numbers in code blocks
    pub line_numbers: bool,
    /// Custom language aliases (e.g., "rs" -> "rust")
    pub language_aliases: HashMap<String, String>,
    /// Languages to never highlight (render as plain)
    pub disabled_languages: HashSet<String>,
}

#[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
impl Default for SyntaxThemeConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            theme: "base16-ocean.dark".to_string(),
            line_numbers: false,
            language_aliases: HashMap::new(),
            disabled_languages: HashSet::new(),
        }
    }
}
```

### 2. Builder API
```rust
impl StyleConfig {
    /// Set the syntax highlighting theme
    #[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
    pub fn syntax_theme(mut self, theme: &str) -> Self {
        self.syntax_theme.theme = theme.to_string();
        self
    }
    
    /// Enable line numbers in code blocks
    #[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
    pub fn with_line_numbers(mut self, enabled: bool) -> Self {
        self.syntax_theme.line_numbers = enabled;
        self
    }
    
    /// Add a custom language alias
    #[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
    pub fn language_alias(mut self, alias: &str, language: &str) -> Self {
        self.syntax_theme.language_aliases
            .insert(alias.to_string(), language.to_string());
        self
    }
    
    /// Disable highlighting for specific language
    #[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
    pub fn disable_language(mut self, lang: &str) -> Self {
        self.syntax_theme.disabled_languages.insert(lang.to_string());
        self
    }
}

// Usage example:
let config = StyleConfig::default()
    .syntax_theme("Solarized (dark)")
    .with_line_numbers(true)
    .language_alias("dockerfile", "docker")
    .disable_language("text");
```

### 3. Theme Validation
```rust
impl StyleConfig {
    /// Validate that the configured theme exists
    #[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), ConfigError> {
        let ts = ThemeSet::load_defaults();
        if !ts.themes.contains_key(&self.syntax_theme.theme) {
            return Err(ConfigError::UnknownTheme {
                name: self.syntax_theme.theme.clone(),
                available: ts.themes.keys().cloned().collect(),
            });
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### 4. Serialization Support
```rust

#[cfg(feature = "serde")]
impl Serialize for SyntaxThemeConfig { /* ... */ }

#[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]

// theme = "Solarized (dark)"
// line_numbers = true
```

### 5. Runtime Theme Switching
```rust
impl Renderer {
    /// Change the syntax theme at runtime
    #[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
    pub fn set_syntax_theme(&mut self, theme: &str) -> Result<(), ConfigError> {
        let ts = ThemeSet::load_defaults();
        let new_theme = ts.themes.get(theme)
            .ok_or(ConfigError::UnknownTheme { /* ... */ })?;
        self.syntax_theme = SyntaxTheme::from_theme(new_theme.clone());
        Ok(())
    }
}
```
